name: Almond
summary: The most vitamin-E-dense common nut, with heart-healthy monounsaturated fat and magnesium.
superfoodReason: One of the richest whole-food sources of vitamin E, combined with monounsaturated fat and magnesium, backed by nut-and-heart trial evidence.

benefits.claim: Top source of vitamin E for skin and cells
benefits.mechanism: Almonds are among the most vitamin-E-dense foods. Vitamin E is a fat-soluble antioxidant that protects cell membranes — including skin cells — from oxidative damage.

citations.title: Vitamin E — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

benefits.mechanism: Almonds' monounsaturated fat, fiber, and plant sterols help lower LDL cholesterol when they replace less-healthy snacks. In the PREDIMED trial, a Mediterranean diet supplemented with nuts reduced cardiovascular events.

citations.title: Primary prevention of cardiovascular disease with a Mediterranean diet supplemented with extra-virgin olive oil or nuts (Estruch et al., PREDIMED, N Engl J Med 2018)

benefits.mechanism: Almonds' fat, fiber, and protein slow how fast the stomach empties and carbohydrates are absorbed, so eating them with or before a carb-rich meal lowers the resulting glucose rise. A dose-response meta-analysis of randomized trials found almond intake improved glycemic control.

citations.title: The effect of almond intake on glycemic control: a systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Asbaghi et al., Phytother Res 2022)

surprisingFacts.text: Whole almonds deliver fewer calories than their label implies — some fat stays locked in the nut's cell walls and passes through undigested, an effect measured in controlled feeding studies.
